---
title: "Machine Learning Foundations"
page-layout: full
---

## Course Overview

INFO 521 is a 7.5-week graduate course covering the foundations of machine learning, from classical linear regression through neural networks. Taught by **Greg Chism**, this course emphasizes both theoretical understanding and practical application, combining interactive peer-engagement loops with projects that integrate the full learning arc.

:::{.grid}

:::{.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Slides

Lecture decks in reveal.js with dark/light toggle and PDF export. Topics span foundations, learning theory, Bayesian methods, model selection, and neural networks.

[Browse Slides →](slides.qmd)
:::

:::{.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Activities

Six weekly peer-engagement loops: interactive tool + mastery quiz → reflection → peer review → revise. Each tool is a self-contained visualization with a retakeable quiz.

[Explore Activities →](activities.qmd)
:::

:::{.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Assessment

Specifications grading — no points, no weighted averages. Eight pass/not-yet deliverables (7 homework units + a Capstone) set your letter on a published ladder; revise-and-resubmit is built in.

[See Grading Scheme →](assessment.qmd)
:::

:::

## Schedule

The course follows a **module-by-module schedule**, each pairing slides with hands-on activities. Review the full schedule and see what's in progress:

[Module Schedule →](schedule.qmd)

---

## Key Resources

- **Homeworks**: Practice problems deployed via [GitHub Classroom]({{< var classroom_url >}})
- **Projects**: Clinical dataset carries the semester arc across two projects
- **Checkpoints**: Retakeable assessments covering key concepts
